Title: Create Go engine skeleton
Description: Initialize the Go module, engine entrypoint, and a minimal JSON stdin/stdout command that can accept a request and emit a structured response
Acceptance: `cmd/markmaton-engine` exists, the Go module builds, and the engine can echo a valid response shape from a minimal request

Title: Define engine request and response models
Description: Define stable Go-side request and response structures for HTML input, URL context, markdown output, metadata, links, images, and quality fields
Acceptance: Go model types exist and match the architecture brief; no fetch or LLM concerns appear in the models

Title: Create Python wrapper skeleton
Description: Add Python-side models and an engine runner abstraction that will later call the Go binary without embedding parser logic in Python
Acceptance: Python wrapper package has clear modules for models, engine invocation, and CLI boundaries

Title: Implement cleanhtml first pass
Description: Implement first-pass HTML cleaning with support for removing non-content nodes and honoring include/exclude selectors
Acceptance: Cleaned HTML removes obvious noise, preserves core content blocks, and stays within parser-core responsibilities
Notes: Phase 2; unit tests should use local HTML fixtures

Title: Implement URL resolution helpers
Description: Implement base URL handling, absolute URL normalization for links and images, and srcset primary image selection
Acceptance: Relative links and images are resolved correctly when URL context is present; behavior is defined when URL is absent

Title: Implement Markdown conversion adapter
Description: Wire in the Markdown conversion core so clean HTML can be transformed into Markdown through one explicit conversion path
Acceptance: A conversion adapter exists, returns Markdown for representative fixtures, and does not pull in fetch or browser logic

Title: Implement Markdown postprocessing
Description: Implement Markdown postprocessing for whitespace cleanup, trailing spaces, and a small set of deterministic fixes
Acceptance: Postprocessing improves output consistency without hiding conversion bugs or mixing in LLM logic

Title: Implement metadata extraction
Description: Implement metadata extraction for title, description, canonical URL, and core page metadata fields
Acceptance: Metadata extraction returns stable fields for representative fixtures and degrades cleanly on sparse HTML

Title: Implement link and image extraction
Description: Implement link and image extraction modules that operate on cleaned HTML and resolved URLs
Acceptance: Links and images are extracted in a predictable shape and reflect resolved URL behavior

Title: Implement quality heuristics
Description: Add lightweight quality signals such as text length, link density, image count, title presence, and a first-pass quality score
Acceptance: Response includes quality fields that are deterministic and useful for judging weak outputs

Title: Implement main-content fallback policy
Description: Add a simple fallback policy that can retry with broader content selection when main-content output is clearly too weak
Acceptance: Fallback decision is explicit, deterministic, and observable in response fields

Title: Add fixture and golden test harness
Description: Create a testdata layout for representative page types and a golden harness for markdown outputs
Acceptance: Fixtures and golden outputs exist for core page shapes and are easy to extend

Title: Add Go unit tests for parser modules
Description: Write focused unit tests for cleanhtml, resolve, convert, postprocess, metadata, links, images, and quality using local fixtures only
Acceptance: Core parser modules have unit tests with clear assertions and no network or browser dependency
Notes: Phase 5; keep tests mocked or fixture-based

Title: Add Python unit tests with mocked engine boundary
Description: Write Python unit tests for models, CLI argument handling, and engine invocation using mocked subprocess boundaries
Acceptance: Python tests cover wrapper behavior without launching the real engine in automated runs
Notes: Phase 5; do not add automated integration tests here

Title: Define binary discovery and packaging layout
Description: Define how the Python package finds, bundles, or locates the Go binary for local development and future PyPI/uv tool distribution
Acceptance: A clear binary discovery contract exists for dev and packaged installs, and the packaging path is explicit instead of implicit
Test_Method: manual: review packaging layout against official uv tool and Hatch wheel docs and confirm the binary path rules are explicit

Title: Define local developer smoke flow
Description: Document a manual smoke path for running the real Go engine against a fixture or sample HTML when deeper validation is needed
Acceptance: A repeatable manual smoke checklist exists for local validation without becoming part of the default automated suite
Test_Method: manual: build ./bin/markmaton-engine and run python3 -m markmaton.cli convert against testdata/fixtures/article.html

Title: Validate implementation bootstrap artifacts
Description: Validate the implementation plan and CSV, and confirm they reflect unit-test-first execution order with manual integration only
Acceptance: Implementation CSV passes validation and the execution order is dependency-safe and aligned with the testing policy
